title: TCP/IP Illustrated, Volume 1: The Protocols
subtitle: The Protocols
author: Kevin R. Fall

coAuthor.name: W. Richard Stevens
knownFor: Author of TCP/IP Illustrated Vol 1 (1994), Vol 2 (1995), Vol 3 (1996)
Author of Advanced Programming in the UNIX Environment (1992)
Author of UNIX Network Programming Vol 1 & 2
otherWorks: Advanced Programming in the UNIX Environment

themes: Bottom-up exposition of the TCP/IP protocol suite from the wire to the application
Packet-level analysis using real network traces as the primary teaching device
RFC-anchored specification reading with the standards as ground truth
The BSD socket API as the canonical interface between applications and protocols
State-machine and timing-diagram analysis of TCP's connection lifecycle
Pragmatic comparison of IPv4 and IPv6 operation on modern dual-stack systems
Performance, reliability, and congestion behavior observed empirically
awards: Acclaimed as the gold-standard reference for TCP/IP practitioners since 1994
Foundational text behind multiple generations of Unix and network engineers
Stevens received the USENIX Lifetime Achievement Award (2000, posthumous)
excerpt: The book that taught an entire industry to read a packet capture. Where most networking texts stop at diagrams, Stevens and Fall hand you the actual bytes on the wire and walk you, byte by byte, through what the kernel and the network actually do.
